# Appendix A: Context Optimizer Skill (SKILL.md)

This appendix provides a reference implementation for a **context optimizer skill** -- a self-contained module that manages token budgets, skill activation, and compression within an AI agent system.

The skill follows the architecture described in Chapter 12 and the token budget principles from Chapter 10.

## SKILL.md Format

Every skill in a context-engineered system is defined by a `SKILL.md` file. This is both human-readable documentation and machine-parseable configuration.

```markdown
# Context Optimizer

## Identity
- Name: context-optimizer
- Tier: core
- Version: 1.0.0

## Triggers
- context
- tokens
- budget
- compress
- optimize

## Description
Manages token budget allocation, skill activation/deactivation,
and context compression. Runs automatically when context usage
exceeds warning thresholds.

## Dependencies
- None (this skill is self-contained)

## Configuration
- max_active_skills: 5
- token_warning_threshold: 12000
- token_critical_threshold: 15000
- auto_deactivate_after: 10  # turns

## Tools Required
- File read (for loading skill content)
- Token counter (for budget tracking)
```

## Reference Implementation

### Skill Loader

The skill loader reads `SKILL.md` files and parses them into a structured format:

```javascript
function parseSkillMd(content) {
  const sections = {};
  let currentSection = null;

  for (const line of content.split("\n")) {
    if (line.startsWith("## ")) {
      currentSection = line.slice(3).trim().toLowerCase();
      sections[currentSection] = [];
    } else if (currentSection && line.trim()) {
      sections[currentSection].push(line.trim());
    }
  }

  return {
    name: extractField(sections, "identity", "Name"),
    tier: extractField(sections, "identity", "Tier"),
    version: extractField(sections, "identity", "Version"),
    triggers: extractList(sections, "triggers"),
    description: sections["description"]?.join(" ") ?? "",
    dependencies: extractList(sections, "dependencies"),
    config: extractKeyValues(sections, "configuration")
  };
}

function extractField(sections, section, field) {
  const line = sections[section]?.find(l => l.startsWith(`- ${field}:`));
  return line?.split(":").slice(1).join(":").trim() ?? null;
}

function extractList(sections, section) {
  return (sections[section] ?? [])
    .filter(l => l.startsWith("- "))
    .map(l => l.slice(2).trim());
}

function extractKeyValues(sections, section) {
  const config = {};
  for (const line of sections[section] ?? []) {
    if (line.startsWith("- ") && line.includes(":")) {
      const [key, ...rest] = line.slice(2).split(":");
      const value = rest.join(":").trim();
      config[key.trim()] = isNaN(value) ? value : Number(value);
    }
  }
  return config;
}
```

### Context Optimizer Core

The optimizer manages the full lifecycle: activation, budget tracking, compression, and deactivation.

```javascript
class ContextOptimizer {
  constructor(skillCatalog, config = {}) {
    this.catalog = skillCatalog;
    this.activeSkills = new Map();
    this.turnCounter = 0;

    this.maxActive = config.max_active_skills ?? 5;
    this.warnAt = config.token_warning_threshold ?? 12000;
    this.criticalAt = config.token_critical_threshold ?? 15000;
    this.deactivateAfter = config.auto_deactivate_after ?? 10;
  }

  // Main entry point: called each turn
  async optimizeContext(userQuery, currentContext) {
    this.turnCounter++;

    // 1. Determine which skills should be active
    const candidates = this.findCandidates(userQuery);

    // 2. Activate new skills (up to limit)
    for (const candidate of candidates) {
      if (!this.activeSkills.has(candidate.name)) {
        await this.activate(candidate);
      } else {
        this.activeSkills.get(candidate.name).lastUsedTurn = this.turnCounter;
      }
    }

    // 3. Deactivate stale skills
    this.deactivateStale();

    // 4. Enforce limit
    this.enforceLimits();

    // 5. Check budget
    const budget = this.calculateBudget(currentContext);

    if (budget.total > this.criticalAt) {
      this.compress(currentContext);
    }

    return {
      activeSkills: [...this.activeSkills.values()],
      budget,
      compressed: budget.total > this.warnAt
    };
  }

  findCandidates(query) {
    const q = query.toLowerCase();
    return this.catalog
      .filter(skill => skill.triggers.some(t => q.includes(t)))
      .sort((a, b) => {
        const tierOrder = { core: 0, standard: 1, optional: 2 };
        return (tierOrder[a.tier] ?? 2) - (tierOrder[b.tier] ?? 2);
      });
  }

  async activate(skill) {
    const content = await loadSkillContent(skill.name);
    this.activeSkills.set(skill.name, {
      ...skill,
      content,
      tokens: estimateTokens(content),
      activatedTurn: this.turnCounter,
      lastUsedTurn: this.turnCounter
    });
  }

  deactivateStale() {
    for (const [name, skill] of this.activeSkills) {
      if (this.turnCounter - skill.lastUsedTurn > this.deactivateAfter) {
        this.activeSkills.delete(name);
      }
    }
  }

  enforceLimits() {
    if (this.activeSkills.size <= this.maxActive) return;

    const sorted = [...this.activeSkills.entries()]
      .sort(([, a], [, b]) => {
        const tierOrder = { core: 0, standard: 1, optional: 2 };
        const tierDiff = (tierOrder[a.tier] ?? 2) - (tierOrder[b.tier] ?? 2);
        if (tierDiff !== 0) return tierDiff;
        return b.lastUsedTurn - a.lastUsedTurn;
      });

    // Keep top N, remove the rest
    const toRemove = sorted.slice(this.maxActive);
    for (const [name] of toRemove) {
      this.activeSkills.delete(name);
    }
  }

  calculateBudget(context) {
    const constitutional = context.constitutionalTokens ?? 2000;
    const catalog = estimateTokens(JSON.stringify(this.catalog));
    const skills = [...this.activeSkills.values()]
      .reduce((sum, s) => sum + s.tokens, 0);
    const memory = context.memoryTokens ?? 0;
    const working = context.workingTokens ?? 0;

    return {
      constitutional,
      catalog,
      skills,
      memory,
      working,
      total: constitutional + catalog + skills + memory + working
    };
  }

  compress(context) {
    // Priority: drop optional skills first, then summarise memory
    const optional = [...this.activeSkills.entries()]
      .filter(([, s]) => s.tier === "optional");

    for (const [name] of optional) {
      this.activeSkills.delete(name);
    }
  }
}
```

### Usage Example

```javascript
// Load catalog at startup (~200-300 tokens)
const catalog = await loadSkillCatalog("skills/catalog.yaml");

// Create optimizer
const optimizer = new ContextOptimizer(catalog);

// Each turn
const result = await optimizer.optimizeContext(
  userQuery,
  { constitutionalTokens: 2000, memoryTokens: 1500, workingTokens: 800 }
);

console.log(`Active: ${result.activeSkills.map(s => s.name).join(", ")}`);
console.log(`Budget: ${result.budget.total} tokens`);
```

## File Structure

A complete context optimizer skill lives in a single directory:

```
skills/
  context-optimizer/
    SKILL.md              # Skill definition (shown above)
    optimizer.js          # Core implementation
    tests/
      optimizer.test.js   # Unit tests
```

## Key Sources

- Chapter 10: Token Budget Architecture (budget allocation)
- Chapter 11: Compression Strategies (compression priority order)
- Chapter 12: Skill Architecture for AI Agents (catalog, activation, deactivation)
- OpenClaw context management (production patterns)
